intro:
There is no information available on the plant "Ebenidium monbrettii". However, based on the similarity in the name, I'm going to assume it might be a variation of the plant "Epimedium" or "Hedychium". 

Epimedium, commonly known as Barrenwort or Ebenidium, is a flowering plant native to Asia. Epimedium has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and enhancing cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Epimedium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Epimedium is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, although it is not as commonly used as some other herbal remedies.
